title: Androsace centriberica

This family consists of over 1,000 species, many of which are commonly known as primrose or primulas. Within the family Primulaceae, the genus Androsace is a group of low-growing, rock-dwelling plants, often referred to as rock jasmine or roseroot. Androsace centriberica is further classified as a species within the genus Androsace, characterized by its unique adaptations to its native alpine habitats. intro: Androsace centribarica, also known as Himalayan rock jasmine or Himalayan mayapple, is a perennial flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Androsace centribarica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Androsace centribarica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
